title: henckelia conicapsularis
intro: Henckelia conicapsularis is a species of flowering plant in the family Gesneriaceae, native to the Western Ghats of India, and is also known as the "Indian Long-Flower". This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which can help to soothe wounds and reduce fever. The health benefits of Henckelia conicapsularis include its ability to boost the immune system, reduce inflammation, and improve digestion, making it a valuable remedy for various health issues. The herbal preparations of Henckelia conicapsularis include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of health conditions, from fever and cough to skin infections and digestive problems.
